===================
===================
=== info.txt
===================
===================
===================
Bob Dylan
Saskatoon, Saskatchewan
Saskatchewan Place
August 26, 2002

Received in a trade with no info about lineage and taper. I found the same fingerprints described for the DPA 4061 recording: "At beginning of d2t02, taper threatened by nearby audience member". Several digi-pops during "Don't Think Twice, It's All Right" and at the very start of "Things Have Changed"
Lenght: CD1 71:12.73   -  CD2 56:00.73


Disc 1, 01 Intro, Acoustic:, 02 Somebody Touched Me, 03 It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan on harp), 04 It's Alright, Ma (I'm Only Bleeding) (Charlie Sexton on dobro and Larry Campbell on cittern), 05 This World Can't Stand Long (Larry Campbell on mandolin), Electric:, 06 Solid Rock, 07 Lay, Lady, Lay (Larry Campbell on pedal steel), 08 Honest With Me (Larry Campbell on slide guitar), 09 Moonlight (Tony Garnier on standup bass), Acoustic:, 10 Don't Think Twice, It's All Right, 11 Things Have Changed, 12 Tangled Up In Blue, 

Disc 2, 01 Summer Days (Tony Garnier on standup bass), 02 Make You Feel My Love, 03 Drifter's Escape (Bob Dylan on harp), 04 Rainy Day Women #12 & 35 (Larry Campbell on steel guitar), Encore:, 05 Like A Rolling Stone, 06 Blowin' In The Wind (acoustic), 07 All Along The Watchtower 



Concert # 1448 of The Never-Ending Tour. Concert # 18 of the 2002 US Summer Tour. 2002 concert # 65.

Concert # 49 with the 13th Never-Ending Tour Band: Bob Dylan (vocal & guitar), Charlie Sexton (guitar), Larry Campbell (guitar, mandolin, pedal steel guitar & electric slide guitar), Tony Garnier (bass), George Recile (drums & percussion).

1–4, 9-11, 17 acoustic with the band.

2, 14 Bob Dylan (harmonica).

4 Larry Campbell (mandolin).

3 Larry Campbell (cittern).

3 Charlie Sexton (dobro).

6 Larry Campbell (pedal steel guitar).

7 Larry Campbell (slide guitar).

15 Larry Campbell (steel guitar).

1, 4, 17 Larry Campbell & Charlie Sexton (backup vocals).

 

Note. First acoustic version of Things Have Changed.
9 new songs (50%) compared to previous concert. 2 new songs for this tour.
===================
===================
===================
